turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Requesting Maintenance or Repairs in a Rental Property

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, I’m calling about a problem in my apartment.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Hi there, thanks for calling. No problem at all, I’m happy to help you today. Can you tell me what kind of problem you’re having in your apartment?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: There is water under the sink in the kitchen.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, thanks for letting me know. Water under the sink can definitely be concerning, so it’s good you called. Is the water leaking right now, or did you just notice it earlier?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: It’s leaking slowly. Not big water, but always wet.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I see, thanks for explaining that. So it’s a slow leak but it’s constant, right? When did you first notice the water under the sink?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Maybe two days ago. But yesterday more water.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. So it started a couple of days ago and then became worse yesterday. That’s helpful information. Can you see where the water is coming from, for example from a pipe or from the sink itself?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Sorry, pipe meaning the silver one?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, exactly, the silver pipe underneath the sink. Thanks for checking. Do you think the water is coming from that pipe or from another part?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, from that pipe. I think from the joint.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, that makes sense. A joint leak is quite common and usually easy for a plumber to fix, so don’t worry too much. Have you tried turning off the water valve under the sink yet?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Water valve… I’m not sure where it is.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s completely okay, many people aren’t sure. Under the sink, you should see a small handle or tap connected to the pipe. Take your time and tell me if you can see something like that.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I see something like handle. Should I turn it now?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, please. You can slowly turn it clockwise. That should stop the water for now. Let me know once you’ve done that.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, I turned it. Now water stop.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Perfect, well done. That’s great. Stopping the water helps prevent any further damage while we organise the repair. Is everything dry now, or is there still some water left under the sink?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Still little water, but no more coming.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s fine. You can wipe that up later. The important thing is that it’s no longer leaking. Now, let’s organise a plumber to fix this properly. Are you usually at home during the week, or do you have specific days that work better for you?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I work from home sometimes. What day is possible?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, that helps. Let me check availability. We have a plumber available tomorrow afternoon or Thursday morning. Tomorrow afternoon would be between 1 and 5 pm. Thursday morning would be between 9 and 12. Which one sounds better for you?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Tomorrow afternoon meaning after lunch?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, exactly, after lunch. Any time from 1 pm onwards. Do you have a preferred time within that window?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I can do after 3 pm. Is that okay?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, that’s absolutely fine. I’ll make a note for after 3 pm. The plumber will aim to arrive in that time frame. Do you want them to call you before they come?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, please. Can plumber call me before coming?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course. I’ll ask the plumber to call you about 30 minutes before arrival so you know exactly when they’re coming. Now, I just want to check: do you need to stay home the whole time, or will you need to go out?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: If I go out shortly, can I leave key?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, that’s fine. You can leave the key with reception or in the key safe if you have one. Just let us know what you prefer, and we’ll pass that information on to the plumber.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. Thank you. Can I ask one more question?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course, take your time. What would you like to ask?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Is this repair free or I need to pay?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s a very common question. If the issue is normal wear and tear, which this sounds like, then there’s no cost for you. We cover the repair.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Wear and tear meaning old problem?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, exactly. Problems that happen naturally over time, not because something was broken on purpose.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. I didn’t break anything.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s completely fine. Based on what you’ve described, it sounds like normal maintenance. If the plumber finds anything unexpected, they’ll let us know and we’ll handle it.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: If plumber find bigger problem, what happen?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good question. If it’s a bigger issue, we’ll contact you and explain the next steps. You won’t need to decide anything on the spot.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. Can you confirm the time again?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, of course. The plumber is scheduled for tomorrow, Wednesday, after 3 pm, and they’ll call you about 30 minutes before arriving.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Tomorrow is Wednesday, right?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, that’s right, Wednesday.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. And plumber will call me before?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, they will. I’ve clearly noted that in the request.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Do I get message or email about this?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, I’ll send you a confirmation email later today with all the details so you have it in writing.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Email meaning today?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, later today. It should arrive within a few hours.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. Can I have reference number also?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, absolutely. Your maintenance reference number is 64218. I’ll say it slowly for you.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Sorry, can you repeat slowly?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Of course. Six… four… two… one… eight.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Six four two one eight. Correct?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, that’s exactly right. You’ve got it.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I will write it down now.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: No problem at all. Take your time.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Just confirm, plumber comes tomorrow after 3 pm, right?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, that’s correct. Tomorrow after 3 pm, with a call before arrival.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. Thank you very much for your help.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You’re very welcome. You did the right thing by calling early. If anything changes or you have more questions, please don’t hesitate to contact us again. Have a great rest of your day.
